summary.intent: Reproduce and fix GitHub issue #278 where Moltis failed to connect to legacy SSE MCP servers (Baserow, NocoDB, lox-mcp) because it skipped the required SSE handshake to discover the session endpoint.
summary.outcome: Implemented a new `LegacySseTransport` that performs the correct 2-step handshake (GET SSE endpoint â†’ discover message URL â†’ POST requests), routed `TransportType::Sse` to use it, and shipped with full test coverage. All 4986 workspace tests pass.

repo: The crate previously conflated two distinct MCP transport protocols: 'Streamable HTTP' (direct POST) was used for both `Sse` and `StreamableHttp` transport types â€” `Sse` was never actually implemented.

Legacy SSE MCP servers require a GET handshake to the SSE endpoint first; they respond with `event: endpoint` containing a message URL (possibly relative: `/message?sessionId=...`, `?sessionId=...`, or absolute) before accepting POST requests.
The MCP crate lives at `crates/mcp/` with transport implementations in `src/sse_transport.rs` (Streamable HTTP) and now `src/legacy_sse_transport.rs` (legacy SSE).
Manager routing for transport types is in `crates/mcp/src/manager.rs`; client connection methods are in `crates/mcp/src/client.rs`.
The workspace uses a pinned nightly Rust toolchain for formatting; format checks must use that pinned version.

code.finding: New file implementing `McpTransport` for legacy SSE protocol: GETs SSE endpoint to discover message URL, resolves relative URLs, caches discovered endpoint for subsequent requests.
Previously had a combined `Sse | StreamableHttp` arm both using `SseTransport`. Split into separate arms: `Sse` â†’ `LegacySseTransport`, `StreamableHttp` â†’ `SseTransport`.

workflow: Compilation check (`cargo check`) before running tests catches errors faster than `cargo test`.
Clippy was run after tests and surfaced two issues in the new file that needed fixing before final commit.
The workflow was: write â†’ compile â†’ test â†’ format â†’ clippy â†’ fix clippy â†’ retest â†’ format verify â†’ commit.

friction: Clippy flagged two issues in the newly written `legacy_sse_transport.rs` that required an extra fix-and-retest cycle.
The root cause required reading through multiple files (sse_transport.rs, client.rs, registry.rs, remote.rs, manager.rs, traits.rs, types.rs, Cargo.toml) before the full picture was clear.
open_items: The PR was committed and pushed but the transcript ends before confirming the PR was actually created on GitHub.
No integration test against a real legacy SSE server (Baserow, NocoDB, lox-mcp) â€” all tests use mocked HTTP servers.
Relative URL resolution for the discovered endpoint message URL may have edge cases not covered by the three test scenarios (absolute, path-relative, query-only).
